english: pressingly, e.g. źú-ba (zhu ba) to request, to solicit Glr.; nán-gyis zar ǰúg-pa (nan gyis zar 'jug pa) to urge, to compel (a person) to eat Dzl.; nán-gyis skór-ba (nan gyis skor ba) to press, to crowd, round Dzl.; nán-gyis gúg-pa (nan gyis gug pa) to make a person come near by calling to him Mil.; nan-čágs (nan chags) 1. sbst. certainty, surety, *da nan-čág tob soṅ* (da nan chag thob song) W. now I have certainty, now I know for sure; nan-čags ṭems? (nan chags thems?) Zam. 2. adv. certainly, surely W., C.; adj. *lon nan-čág* (lon nan chag) W. certain news. — nán-tan (nan tan) 1. sbst. earnest desire, application, exertion Cs.; byaṅ-čúb-la nán-tan byéd-pa (byang chub la nan tan byed pa) to strive earnestly for perfection Dzl.; nán-tan-du byéd-pa (nan tan du byed pa) Thgy.; in čós-kyi nán-tan ysuṅs (chos kyi nan tan gsungs) Pth. 'kyi' (kyi) is perh. to be cancelled. 2. adv. C.: certainly, positively, *ṅe nén-ten láb-pa* (nge nen ten lab pa), *nen-čág zér-pa* (nen chag zer pa), I have told him so definitively, as my unalterable decision; W.: earnestly, ardently, accurately, *nán-tan źib-ča ltos* (nan tan zhib cha ltos) look at it, examine it, accurately! *nán-tan čos* (nan tan chos) do it well, most carefully! *nán-tan srág-če* (nan tan srag che) to burn entirely. — nán-tar (nan tar) very, nán-tar bzaṅ (nan tar bzang) Lex.; very much, all the more, altogether Mil.; nan-túr (nan tur), of rare occurrence, = nán-tan (nan tan). — nón-pa (non pa), mnán-pa (mnan pa) are cog. to nan (nan).
1. Ts. for ná-ba (na ba) sick, ill. — 2. W. *ču nán-te kyoṅ* (chu nan te khyong), for ran-te (ran te), ḍren-te ('dren te), conduct the water this way!
W. late, recent, what has happened a few weeks or months ago.
put on (your clothes)! Sch., v. mnáb-pa (mnab pa).
one of the lunar mansions, v. rgyu-skár (rgyu skar) S.

I. sbst. 1. night, nam láṅs-te (nam langs te), or -nas (-nas), when night departs, at day-break, frq.; nam-gáṅ (nam gang) Sch.: the last day of the lunar month on which there is no moon-shine at all; nam-gúṅ (nam gung) midnight, nám-gyi gúṅ-tun-la (nam gyi gung thun la) in the hour of midnight Dom.; nam-stód (nam stod) the first half of the night, nam-smád (nam smad) the second half of the night; nám-gyi ča stod (nam gyi cha stod), smad (smad), id. — nam-pyéd (nam phyed) midnight Dzl., Glr.; nam-żóṅ (?) (nam zhong) Sch. in the morning; nam-ríṅ (nam ring) Sch. a long day (??) — nam-láṅs (nam langs) day-break, nam-laṅs-kyi-bar-du (nam langs kyi bar du) Dzl. — nam-sród (nam srod) darkness of night, nam-sród byiṅ soṅ-bai tse (nam srod bying song bai tshe) as it was almost quite dark Mil., *nam-sród yol soṅ-nas* (nam srod yol song nas) Sch. id. — 2. for nam-mka (nam mkha') q. v. II. adv. of time, also dus-nám-źig (dus nam zhig), 1. when? frq., how long a time? seldom; rgyún-du nam ǰi ča med sgom (rgyun du nam ji cha med sgom) always keep in mind that you do not know when you will die Mil.; dus-nám-źig-gi tsé-nas (dus nam zhig gi tshe nas) since when? since what time? how long ago? Mil.; relatively: nam ʽgró-bai dus byéd-pa (nam 'gro bai dus byed pa) to appoint the time, when one is going to start Dzl.; nam źig sgyú-lus ʽǰóg-pai tse (nam zhig sgyu lus 'jog pai tshe), when he shall lay aside his phantom-body Mil.; *nam tsúg-pa ko ma léb-na, de tug*... (*nam tshug pa kho ma leb na, de thug*...), as long as he has not come, so long . . . W. — 2. nám(-du) yaṅ (nam (-du) yang) (col. *nám-aṅ* (nam ang), *náms-aṅ* (nams ang)) with a negative, never, in sentences relating to the past, or the future, or containing a prohibition, cf. mi (mi) and ma (ma), nam-yaṅ mi zín-to (nam yang mi zin to) it will never be finished Dzl. 2 S, 9; sṅon nam yaṅ ma tos (sngon nam yang ma thos) (that) has never been heard of formerly; without a negative in B. rarely, col. frq.: always; nam żag brtan (nam zhag brtan) Mil.; *nám-źag gyún-du* (nam zhag rgyun du) C. id.

(cf. mka (mkha') and ynam (gnam)) the space or region above us, heaven, sky, where the birds are flying, and the saints are soaring, where it lightens and thunders etc.; the ether, as the fifth element S. g.; the principle of expansion and enlargement Wdn.; nám-mka daṅ mnyám-pa (nam mkha' dang mnyam pa) like unto the heavens, as to wide expanse, frq.; inaccurately also for an innumerable multitude, nám-mka daṅ mnyám-pai séms-čan-rnams (nam mkha' dang mnyam pai sems can rnams) Mil.; nám-mkai dbyiṅs (nam mkha'i dbyings), nám-mka-ldiṅ(-mo) (nam mkha' lding (-mo)) v. sub mka (mkha'); nám-mkai mtoṅs (nam mkha'i mthongs) celestial vault, firmament Glr., S. O.; nám-mka-mdog (nam mkha' mdog) the blue colour of the sky, azure; it is supposed to be produced by the southern side of mount Rirab, which consists entirely of azur-stone, Mil.; kyím-gyi nám-mka-la (khyim gyi nam mkha' la) in the air above the house, like bar-snáṅ-la (bar snang la), Tar. 2 S, 2; nam-p̣áṅs ɣčód-pa (nam 'phangs gcod pa), also nam-
